docs: Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam served as the Chief Scientific Adviser to the Government of India and led the Pokhran-II nuclear tests in 1998.
Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam became the 11th President of India in 2002 and served until 2007.
Dr. Vikram Sarabhai founded the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) in 1969 and is considered the father of the Indian space programme.
Dr. Vikram Sarabhai passed away in 1971. After his death, Dr. Satish Dhawan took over as Chairman of ISRO in 1972.
ISRO successfully launched Chandrayaan-1 in 2008, which confirmed the presence of water molecules on the Moon.
ISRO's Chandrayaan-3 mission successfully landed on the south pole of the Moon on August 23, 2023, making India the first country to achieve this feat.
queries: Who is the father of the Indian space programme?
